title: Tool Choice, Evidence, Actions, and Approval Boundaries · Evaluation Brief: Read Before Write
summary: Design a tool-using investigation that reads evidence before changing state. Planned artifact: Add and verify the prompt read before write increment in a safe tool-use decision protocol. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Sequence read-only discovery before mutations unless the user explicitly requests an immediate safe action.
Write tool descriptions and schemas that make capability, inputs, outputs, and side effects unambiguous.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Predict Read Before Write
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt tools actions. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt tools actions. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Before reading, predict which action preserves the Read Before Write contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for sequence read-only discovery before mutations unless the user explicitly requests an immediate safe action.

content.text: Sequence read-only discovery before mutations unless the user explicitly requests an immediate safe action. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: prompt tools actions. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Read Before Write, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a safe tool-use decision protocol.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: An agent should change state to learn what the state is. This fails the Read Before Write contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.value: Anthropic Prompting Best Practices (Current Claude model guidance) — Controls clear instructions, examples, structure, reasoning, tools, and model-specific migration cautions.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Sequence read-only discovery before mutations unless the user explicitly requests an immediate safe action.

steps.title: Build Read Before Write evidence
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-read-before-write-152vxs" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Read Before Write into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-read-before-write-152vxs
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.title: Defend the Read Before Write model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Read Before Write applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Sequence read-only discovery before mutations unless the user explicitly requests an immediate safe action.
